$schema-note: Per-code rationale strings used by fm-validate --explain. Each entry MUST carry severity_hint + what + why + fix. Codes mirror SPEC §5.1 and the F.T.* type-check family added by Task 019 ST-5.

F.3.1.what: One or more L1 frontmatter keys are missing (type / status / slug / summary / created / updated).
F.3.1.why: L1 keys are the universal contract every operational file declares. Missing them blocks every cross-file query and lifecycle audit.
F.3.1.fix: Add the missing keys with valid values. Use `fm-fix --apply` for mechanically-derivable values; otherwise hand-author.

F.3.2.what: One or more L2 keys required by the file's `type:` are missing.
F.3.2.why: Per-type L2 keys are the typed routing surface (e.g. tasks declare task_uses_prompts so prompts can be reverse-resolved). Missing them breaks the reciprocity graph.
F.3.2.fix: Add the listed keys. List-typed L2 keys may use `key: []` if the value is genuinely empty; scalar L2 keys must be authored.

F.3.3.what: Frontmatter is missing entirely, malformed, or its `type:` disagrees with the path-classification table.
F.3.3.why: Frontmatter is the structural contract. A missing or wrong `type:` means every type-specific check below is invalid.
F.3.3.fix: Open the file; re-emit a valid `---`-fenced YAML block with at minimum the L1 keys. If `type:` disagrees, set it to the path-expected value listed in `maintenance/schemas/header-ontology.json`.

F.3.4.what: An unknown frontmatter key's name is within Optimal String Alignment distance 1 of a required key (a likely typo, e.g. `tpye` for `type`).
F.3.4.why: Typos silently shadow the intended key — the validator would otherwise pass a file whose intent is broken.
F.3.4.fix: Inspect manually: rename if a typo, otherwise add to the recognised key set in the ontology. fm-fix refuses to auto-rename — renaming might lose the original value.

F.4.2.what: A `## ` heading required by the file's type is absent or normalises to a different name.
F.4.2.why: Required headings are the bodies' navigation surface. Tools and reviewers expect to find them at predictable names.
F.4.2.fix: Author the missing heading (`## Goal`, `## Plan`, `## Todo`, `## Links` for tasks) and a body that satisfies the §12 body_schema for that section.

F.B.1.what: A section's body shape (paragraph / ordered_list / unordered_list / task_list / link_list / gherkin_block / code_block / definition_list) does not match the §12 body_schema for the file's type.
F.B.1.why: Body-shape mismatches mean downstream tools (fm-section --check-task, the coherence-check linter) cannot operate on the section.
F.B.1.fix: Re-author the body to match the expected shape. If the existing shape is intentional, file a Task to amend the body_schema.

F.B.2.what: A list-shaped section has fewer items than `min_items` or more than `max_items`.
F.B.2.why: Bounds keep the section navigable and stops drift (e.g. an empty Plan is meaningless).
F.B.2.fix: Add or remove items to fall within the bounds.

F.B.3.what: A list item does not match the `item_pattern` regex for the section's body_schema (e.g. a Constraints bullet missing an RFC 2119 keyword).
F.B.3.why: Item patterns enforce normative-keyword discipline (lang-spec U1).
F.B.3.fix: Rewrite the item to include the required pattern (typically MUST / MUST NOT / SHOULD / MAY / etc.).

F.B.11.what: A SKILL.md frontmatter `skill_kind` value is not in the closed 9-value enum {domain, tool, orchestrator, meta, discipline, workflow, persona, analysis, agent-template}.
F.B.11.why: Index routing, manifest emission, and discipline-gate dispatch all depend on `skill_kind` being a known label. Out-of-enum values silently bypass routing. Ratified by Task 094 ST-1 (carry-forward T3 from Task 092 PR #120 review A1).
F.B.11.fix: Either change `skill_kind` to one of the 9 enum values (see SKILLS.md §3.3) or, if a new category is genuinely required, open an ADR to ratify the enum expansion before adding the value.

F.T.1.what: A list-valued L2 field references a slug or task_id that has no matching operational file.
F.T.1.why: Dangling references silently break the reciprocity graph and produce orphaned navigation when agents follow links.
F.T.1.fix: Either author the missing target file, fix the typo'd slug, or remove the dangling reference.

F.T.2.what: A reciprocal pairing (e.g. task_uses_prompts vs prompt_relates_to_task) is asymmetric — one side names the other but the other does not name back.
F.T.2.why: Reciprocity is the integrity invariant for the slug graph. Asymmetry means a reverse query returns wrong results.
F.T.2.fix: Decide which side is correct and fix the other to match. fm-edit --append-list or --remove-from-list applies the change atomically.

ADR.A.1.4.what: An ADR's '## Considered Options' section lists fewer than two items.
ADR.A.1.4.why: Exploration MUST compare at least two mutually exclusive options; a single-option ADR is a foregone conclusion masquerading as a decision (spec §3 Aspect 1 — Explore).
ADR.A.1.4.fix: Add at least one alternative option in '## Considered Options' before re-validating.

ADR.A.2.1.what: An ADR body is missing a MADR 4.0.0 required heading (Context and Problem Statement / Decision Drivers / Considered Options / Decision Outcome / Consequences).
ADR.A.2.1.why: MADR is the lingua franca of ADR tooling. Missing headings break log4brains-style renderers and the agency-adr extractor.
ADR.A.2.1.fix: Author the missing heading. Use templates/adr/ once it lands; in the meantime mirror the structure of an existing Accepted ADR.

ADR.A.2.2.what: ADR frontmatter fails the JSON-Schema in maintenance/schemas/header-ontology.json under types.adr.json_schema.
ADR.A.2.2.why: The schema is the binding contract for the adr_* L2 namespace; violations mean downstream tools cannot trust any field.
ADR.A.2.2.fix: Inspect the diagnostic's location pointer; fix the listed key/value to match the schema (e.g. adr_id MUST match ^ADR-[0-9]{4}$).

ADR.A.2.3.what: An ADR's '## Decision Outcome' section is empty.
ADR.A.2.3.why: The Decision Outcome MUST declare the chosen option in a single sentence; without it the synthesis pipeline has nothing to compress.
ADR.A.2.3.fix: Add a one-sentence statement of the chosen option (optionally a Y-Statement for token-cheap rationale).

ADR.A.2.4.what: An ADR's '## Consequences' section is empty.
ADR.A.2.4.why: Consequences MUST enumerate positive, negative, and neutral impacts so reviewers can audit trade-offs.
ADR.A.2.4.fix: List bullets prefixed 'Positive:', 'Negative:', and (optionally) 'Neutral:' describing each impact.

ADR.A.2.7.what: An ADR file's name disagrees with its frontmatter adr_id and/or slug.
ADR.A.2.7.why: Filename ↔ frontmatter coupling catches rename-without-update drift; the same pattern guards tasks/<NNN>-slug/task.md.
ADR.A.2.7.fix: Either rename the file to match the adr_id and slug, or amend the frontmatter to match the filename. Use fm-rename for slug renames so reciprocal references update atomically.

ADR.A.3.3.what: Synthesis would emit more tokens than --token-limit (default 2000).
ADR.A.3.3.why: The guarded section is read by every agent on every invocation; an unbounded section pollutes context and degrades downstream prompts.
ADR.A.3.3.fix: Deprecate or supersede older ADRs that no longer carry weight, or raise --token-limit with a justification in the PR description (and document the new floor in OQ.3).

ADR.A.3.4.what: The fidelity score under the selected --fidelity-mode dropped below --fidelity-floor (default 0.95).
ADR.A.3.4.why: Fidelity is the integrity check that the compressed body still represents every Accepted ADR's normatives; a low score means the synthesis silently dropped or paraphrased a rule.
ADR.A.3.4.fix: Re-author the most-recently-added ADR's Decision Outcome / Consequences using verbatim BCP-14 sentences; rerun synthesize. If the floor is genuinely too aggressive, lower --fidelity-floor with a justification.

ADR.A.3.5.what: AGENTS.md is missing the <!-- BEGIN AGENCY-ADR SYNTHESIS --> / <!-- END AGENCY-ADR SYNTHESIS --> markers.
ADR.A.3.5.why: The synthesis pipeline refuses to write outside the markers; their absence would force a destructive overwrite, which the spec prohibits.
ADR.A.3.5.fix: Restore both markers in AGENTS.md (the canonical placement is the '## Synthesised ADR Constraints' section near the bottom). The implementation Task seeds these markers; if AGENTS.md was rewritten by hand, copy them back from `git log AGENTS.md`.

ADR.A.4.5.what: The supersession DAG contains a cycle (one ADR transitively supersedes itself).
ADR.A.4.5.why: Cycles induce infinite recursion in the conflict-resolution traversal; they are the only correctness invariant the synthesis pipeline genuinely cannot recover from.
ADR.A.4.5.fix: Inspect the cycle node list in the diagnostic; remove or invert the supersession edge that closes the loop. Typically this means setting one ADR's adr_status back to Accepted and clearing its adr_supersedes.

ADR.A.4.6.what: A supersession edge is asymmetric — one ADR names another in adr_supersedes but the other's adr_superseded_by does not name back.
ADR.A.4.6.why: Reciprocity guarantees the DAG is queryable from either direction; asymmetry means a reverse-supersession query returns wrong results.
ADR.A.4.6.fix: Add the missing reciprocal entry. fm-edit --append-list adds an item to a list-valued frontmatter key atomically (locks + body-byte invariant).

ADR.A.5.4.what: An ADR's frontmatter is missing one of the required keys (type / adr_id / adr_status / …).
ADR.A.5.4.why: These keys are the schema contract; missing them means corpus discovery, supersession traversal, or schema validation cannot run.
ADR.A.5.4.fix: Add the listed keys with valid values. adr_id MUST be ADR-NNNN; adr_status MUST be one of Proposed | Accepted | Superseded | Deprecated.

ADR.A.5.6.what: Two or more ADR files share the same adr_id.
ADR.A.5.6.why: adr_id is the primary key for the corpus; duplicates collapse the DAG nodes and corrupt every supersession query.
ADR.A.5.6.fix: Renumber the more-recently-created ADR to the next free ADR-NNNN slot; update its filename and slug to match.

ADR.A.5.7.what: An ADR references another ADR (via adr_supersedes or adr_superseded_by) whose file does not exist in /decisions/.
ADR.A.5.7.why: Orphaned references break the DAG and silently exclude the missing ADR from the synthesis filter.
ADR.A.5.7.fix: Either restore the referenced file, fix the typo'd reference, or remove the entry if it was an accidental leftover from a draft.
